news.headline: Zelensky refuses Trump admin's request for 50% of Ukraine's mineral wealth, citing lack of security guarantees.
news.summary: The American proposal was criticized by President Volodymyr Zelensky for lacking security guarantees.

news.headline: Coinbase aims to surpass Tether with $86B target for USDC to become top stablecoin issuer in the US. CEO Brian Armstrong emphasized the goal.
news.summary: CEO Brian Armstrong of Coinbase expressed the company's goal to surpass Tether as the leading stablecoin issuer by promoting USDC to the top spot. Armstrong highlighted the intention to elevate USDC’s status to number one in the stablecoin market.

news.headline: Iran suspends flights to Lebanon citing security concerns, reported by IRNA.

PPL misses EPS estimates, raises infrastructure investment plan to $20B. Revenue at $2.211B, EPS 34 cents. Electric sales up 1%. Operating income at $377M. Cash flow at $2.34B. CEO mentions $3B in investments. Dividend increased by 6%.
news.summary: PPL Corporation's (NYSE:PPL) stock price declined following the company's announcement of a fourth-quarter FY24 operating revenue growth of $2.211 billion, surpassing the consensus forecast of $2.048 billion. The adjusted EPS for the quarter was reported at 34 cents, falling short of the expected 37 cents. Electricity sales saw a 1% year-over-year increase, with the PA Regulated segment experiencing a 1.4% rise and the KY Regulated segment up by 0.6% Y/Y. The company's operating income decreased to $377 million from $390 million in the previous year's quarter. PPL's net cash provided by operating activities for the year totaled $2.34 billion, up from $1.758 billion a year ago. CEO Vincent Sorgi highlighted that PPL completed over $3 billion in infrastructure investments. Additionally, PPL announced a 6% increase in its dividend. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's Health Secretary with a 52-48 Senate vote, facing health challenges.
news.summary: In a decision on Thursday, the U.S. Senate has approved Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the head of the Department of Health and Human Services under President Donald Trump. The vote resulted in 52 senators in favor and 48 against, with Sen. Mitch McConnell being the sole Republican who opposed the nomination. Notably, all Democratic senators voted against Kennedy's confirmation. As the newly appointed health secretary, Kennedy will be responsible for overseeing key health agencies such as the FDA, CDC, and CMS. He steps into this role at a time when healthcare expenses are on the rise and cases of bird flu are surfacing amongst both wild birds and poultry farms.

news.headline: TransUnion reports $1.037 billion Q4 revenue, $792 million from US markets, $245 million internationally. $500 million buyback announced.
news.summary: TransUnion (TRU) announced fourth-quarter revenue of $1.037 billion, a 9% increase year-over-year (Y/Y) on a constant currency basis, surpassing the expected $1.027 billion consensus. Revenue from U.S. Markets grew to $792 million, an 8% Y/Y increase, while International revenue saw an 11% Y/Y rise to $245 million. Adjusted EBITDA climbed 16% Y/Y to $378 million, resulting in an EBITDA margin of 36%, up from 34% in the previous year. The adjusted EPS of $0.97 exceeded the anticipated $0.96. Operating cash flow for the year amounted to $832.5 million, with cash and cash equivalents totaling $679 million as of December 31, 2024. Chris Cartwright, President and CEO, highlighted the high single-digit growth of U.S. Markets in the fourth quarter, driven by factors like mortgage pricing, non-mortgage Financial Services growth improvement, and Insurance sector strength. The International segment also showed significant growth.

news.headline: Equinix misses Q4 earnings ($7.92 FFO vs $8.09 est, $2.26B revenue vs $2.27B est), raises cash dividend by 10%. Q1 revenue outlook $2.191-$2.231B, 2025 revenue $9.033-$9.133B. Analysts maintain price forecast at $975. Headline guidance weak but results meet expectations.
news.summary: Equinix, Inc. (NASDAQ: EQIX) recently saw analysts adjusting their price forecast after falling short on fourth-quarter earnings. The company's reported funds from operations (FFO) of $7.92 missed the consensus of $8.09, alongside revenue coming in at $2.26 billion versus the expected $2.27 billion. The Board of Directors responded by increasing the quarterly cash dividend by 10% to $4.69 per share on common stock. Looking ahead, Equinix anticipates first-quarter revenue ranging from $2.191 billion to $2.231 billion, below the estimated $2.285 billion. Their 2025 revenue projection of $9.033 billion-$9.133 billion is also lower than the anticipated $9.404 billion, with an expected AFFO per share of $36.69-$37.51. An analyst from Oppenheimer, Timothy Horan, revised the price forecast from $950 to $975 with an Outperform rating. Despite weak headline guidance, adjustments for factors like FX, Power, and bare metal shutdowns normalized the results to meet expectations at around 7%-8%.

news.headline: West Pharmaceutical stock falls due to weaker 2025 guidance. Q4 EPS at $1.82, sales at $748.8M. Organic growth at 4.5%. Biologics unit sales rise, Pharma unit sees growth in Westar products.
news.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. reported a fourth-quarter adjusted EPS of $1.82, higher than the consensus of $1.72. Their sales reached $748.8 million, surpassing the consensus of $740.47 million. The Proprietary Products Segment recorded a sales increase of 3.4% to $613.9 million with organic net sales growth at 4.5%. High-value products represented 74% of segment net sales, primarily due to demand for self-injection device platforms. The Biologics market unit had high-single-digit organic net sales growth, while the Pharma market unit saw mid-single-digit growth. The company earned around $25 million in customer incentives for HVP Delivery Devices.

news.headline: Biogen's adjusted EPS is $3.44, up 17%, and sales are $2.46 billion, up 2% YoY. Analysts are cautious on growth prospects.
news.summary: Biogen Inc (NASDAQ:BIIB) announced a fourth-quarter adjusted EPS of $3.44, representing a 17% increase, surpassing the expected $3.35. Sales for the quarter reached $2.46 billion, marking a 2% rise year over year in constant currency and a 3% rise on a reported basis, higher than the anticipated $2.40 billion. Analysts express concerns about challenges in the multiple sclerosis segment, noting that while Leqembi sales are strong globally and Skyclarys is poised for international growth, changes in Medicare Part D will impact U.S. sales, reducing revenue by an estimated $15–$30 million. Predictions for 2025 revenue have been adjusted downwards, influencing longer-term forecasts. Analysts urge caution, waiting for clear indications of growth from Biogen's pipeline, existing drugs, or business expansions. Needham maintains a Hold rating on the stock. Ananda Ghosh from HC Wainwright further analyzes the situation.

news.headline: CVS Health's Q4 sales $97.71B beat consensus, total revenue up 4.2%. Analyst upgrades stock with strong 2025 guidance.
news.summary: CVS Health Corp reported fourth-quarter sales of $97.71 billion exceeding the expected $97.19 billion. Total revenues rose by 4.2%, with growth in Health Care Benefits and Pharmacy & Consumer Wellness segments, but a decline in Health Services. The Medical benefit ratio surged from 88.5% to 94.8% due to increased utilization and Medicare Advantage star rating decline. Analysts praised the results and CVS's strong 2025 guidance. Health Services and Pharmacy & Consumer revenues surpassed projections but Health Care Benefits fell short. The medical benefit ratio is expected to improve by 1 percentage point, reaching at least 91.5%.

news.headline: Reddit, GoDaddy & Trade Desk among top 11 losers last week. WST tumbled 33.22%, TTD dipped 31.66%, and FIS fell 17.31%.
news.summary: Eleven large-cap stocks saw significant declines last week. Among them, West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. (NYSE:WST) witnessed a 33.22% drop after releasing its fourth-quarter results and giving a weaker-than-expected 2025 guidance. The Trade Desk, Inc. (NASDAQ:TTD) also faced a 31.66% decrease following disappointing 2024 fiscal-year fourth-quarter earnings and lowered price forecasts by analysts. Fidelity National Information Services, Inc. (NYSE:FIS) experienced a 17.31% decline post its fourth-quarter results announcement. Investors are urged to review if these stocks are part of their portfolio.

news.headline: Bitcoin stays steady amid ETF outflows, SEC hack plea. GameStop eyes entry.
news.summary: The price of Bitcoin has remained relatively stable over the past week, with investors withdrawing funds from American ETFs. There are reports suggesting that GameStop may consider investing in Bitcoin. This comes at a time when Bitcoin is trading at approximately 60,000 USD. The lack of price movement in Bitcoin is attributed to the shift in investor focus towards ETFs and potential new investments in the market.

news.headline: Achieving control, not just ownership, is crucial in Web3 with ownership alone insufficient in the decentralized era.
news.summary: In the realm of Web3, the aim is to have control rather than solely ownership. Merely possessing something is inadequate in the decentralized landscape ahead.

news.headline: Ledn, a Bitcoin lender, faced debanking several times but sees hope under President Trump as crypto gains traction.
news.summary: Ledn, a company in the cryptocurrency industry, is observing positive indicators amidst the new era of digital currencies under the leadership of President Donald Trump. The firm believes that the trend of debanking, often faced by many companies in the sector, may be witnessing a promising conclusion. This potential shift is reflective of a larger transformation within the industry, indicating a more stable and robust environment for cryptocurrencies.

news.headline: Meta gears up to involve Trump in battling EU regulations; stay tuned for further updates.
PM Netanyahu: Israel, US will finish the job on Iran
AI agents, autonomous programs, are revolutionizing crypto with fraud detection and optimized trading, reshaping the industry.
news.summary: Artificial intelligence agents are revolutionizing the cryptocurrency industry by incorporating intelligent automation, enhancing fraud detection capabilities, and improving trading efficiency. These AI agents have been instrumental in streamlining operations and significantly improving accuracy. The infusion of AI technology has led to a reduction in fraudulent activities by approximately 40%, while also boosting trading performance by 25%. This shift towards AI-driven solutions is fundamentally reshaping how cryptocurrency is managed and traded in the market.

news.headline: Texas Bitcoin reserve hearing is considered a symbolic move for crypto, as analysts suggest Fed's policy influences Bitcoin most.
news.summary: Analysts informed Cointelegraph that the Federal Reserve's monetary policy continues to be the primary factor influencing Bitcoin's direction, despite the positive advancement in the hearing.

news.headline: EU Member of Parliament Sarah Knafo fights for Bitcoin to become legal tender in Europe, criticizes ECB's stance. ECB refuses adding digital assets to monetary frameworks, while Federal Reserve promotes cryptocurrencies. Knafo emphasizes BTC as a response to European inflation.
news.summary: In the first week of his presidency, Trump signed an executive order evaluating a digital assets stockpile. The Federal Reserve rejected a digital currency, supporting cryptocurrency growth. The European Central Bank opposed digital assets in monetary frameworks, with MEP Sarah Knafo criticizing ECB's stance. Knafo, a member of France's Reconquête party, highlighted Europe’s missed revolution with Bitcoin. A response to Europe's inflation, Bitcoin counters monetary creation from public deficits per Knafo. ECB viewed as "totalitarian" by her, as traditional currencies under scrutiny for poor political choices.

news.headline: GCL merges with RF Acquisition Corp., starts trading on Nasdaq as "GCL," marks listing with bell ringing.
news.summary: GCL & RF Acquisition Corp. have finalized their merger, marking the commencement of GCL trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “GCL.” The company has also announced a bell ringing ceremony to celebrate its listing. This development signifies a significant milestone for the company post-merger and sets the stage for its future growth in the stock market.

news.headline: Laser tech used for combating climate change, reducing CO2 emissions by 40%, aiming for 1 trillion tons captured.
Dems urged to tap into DOGE spirit as cryptocurrency rallies sharply; it hit $0.70 with a 24-hour volume exceeding $16 billion.
Designing a neurodiverse city can create a more inclusive environment for 1 in 59 children on the autism spectrum.
Stock market near highs, Dow Jones Futures show upward trend. AI giants Amazon, Broadcom close to buy points.
news.summary: The stock market has surged close to its previous highs, with Dow Jones Futures indicating being at a crucial level. Leading AI companies such as Amazon and Broadcom are approaching potential buy points. Investors are advised to closely monitor these stocks and consider their next move at this pivotal moment in the market.

news.headline: GameStop-linked crypto surges 50% due to CEO Ryan Cohen's photo with Michael Saylor. Trading volume reaches $1.83 million.
news.summary: This past week was filled with excitement in the cryptocurrency world, with various notable events taking place. One of the highlights was the surge of GameStop-themed cryptocurrencies, particularly one based on Ethereum, which saw a significant increase of almost 50% in value within a 24-hour period. Its trading volume also spiked by 37% to reach $1.83 million. Another major development was the backing of Bitcoin and Ethereum by a project supported by former President Donald Trump, World Liberty Financial. This news had a positive impact on the crypto market, further adding to the buzz surrounding cryptocurrencies. For more detailed information, you can refer to the full stories on these events.

news.headline: Traders anticipate Ether growth with options market favoring bullish sentiment despite downside risks under $2,600.
news.summary: According to a Nansen research analyst, the ether option markets are currently showing a potential bias towards bullish sentiment, with a hint of caution observed among major investors. The analyst highlighted the presence of notable downside volatility for ETH below the $2,600 mark.

news.headline: Learn how to tokenize real-world assets on Bitcoin, creating digital versions for secure transactions and ownership.
news.summary: Learn about the process of converting physical assets into digital tokens on the Bitcoin blockchain. This involves creating virtual representations of real-world assets to enable secure transactions and establish ownership rights. This method allows for efficient trading and management of assets on the blockchain network.

news.headline: Dollar forwards surging pose risks for Asian central banks as they navigate economic challenges.
IPC and LPG sign strategic agreement, enhancing services for independent pharmacies nationwide. (Character count: 87)
news.summary: On February 16, 2025, in Sun Prairie, Wisconsin, Independent Pharmacy Cooperative (IPC) revealed a significant partnership with Legacy Pharmacy Group (LPG), a move viewed as groundbreaking in the pharmaceutical industry. IPC, the primary Group Purchasing Organization (GPO) for independent pharmacies across the country, announced this collaboration with LPG. This partnership is set to revolutionize operations and improve services for numerous independent pharmacies.

news.headline: McConnell criticizes Trump's tariff plans, worries about trade war and inflation. Powell firm on inflation stance. And Trudeau slams tariffs.
news.summary: The past week witnessed a flurry of economic and political events that greatly impacted the markets. Senator Mitch McConnell criticized President Trump's tariff plans, expressing concerns about the possibility of a trade war and the resulting inflation in prices for American consumers and businesses. McConnell's warnings were conveyed through an op-ed in Louisville's Courier-Journal. Trump, a strong advocate for tariffs, had recently imposed significant import duties on Canada, Mexico, and China, in addition to levying extra tariffs on steel. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell also made headlines with his resolute stance on addressing inflation concerns. All these events collectively made for an eventful week in the realms of politics and economics.

news.headline: KIP Protocol CEO states no SOL to be used beyond Project Viva La Libertad endorsed by Javier Milei.
news.summary: Julian Peh, the CEO of KIP Protocol, stated that no SOL would be used for anything other than operating Project Viva La Libertad.

news.headline: Tether collaborating with US officials to influence stablecoin regulations. Market cap exceeds $142B, highest in the industry.
news.summary: Tether, as reported by CoinMarketCap, boasts a market capitalization exceeding $142 billion, solidifying its position as the leading issuer of stablecoins in the market.

news.headline: President of Argentina under fire, faces impeachment for backing $107M Libra rug pull leading to financial disaster for investors.
news.summary: The President of Argentina is encountering substantial political resistance as a result of his support for a token that led to a financial disaster for investors.

news.headline: Trump's plan for one-for-one tariffs could harm global economy by raising inflation and creating uncertainty.
news.summary: The White House has initiated the process of imposing reciprocal tariffs on American trading partners, causing uncertainty and potentially leading to inflation. This decision may impact various industries and consumers, with potential consequences for the economy.

news.headline: Despite negative reviews, "Captain America: Brave New World" tops box office with $84M in North America.
news.summary: The film "Captain America: Brave New World" was projected to earn approximately $84 million during its opening weekend in North America.

news.headline: Pet parrots sparked a conflict in an NYC building over noise complaints, resulting in a $750k settlement and ruined friendship.
news.summary: A dispute over noise complaints within a Manhattan co-op resulted in a substantial $750,000 legal settlement and ultimately ended a friendship. The conflict arose from complaints regarding noise disturbances within the building, leading to a legal battle that culminated in the significant settlement amount. The disagreement not only had financial implications but also irreparably damaged a once-close friendship.

news.headline: Analyst forecasts revenue opportunities for CoreCivic, Inc. with impressive Q4 results: $479.3M revenue, beating estimates, and 39 cents per share FFO, exceeding analyst projections. Predicts 2025 net income $53.5M-$67.5M and FFO $1.37-$1.50 per share.
news.summary: Research analyst Jason Weaver reiterated a Buy rating and a price forecast of $28 for CoreCivic, Inc. (NYSE:CXW) based on their fourth-quarter results. CoreCivic reported revenue of $479.3 million, surpassing analyst estimates of $465.17 million. The funds from operations (FFO) were 39 cents per share, beating analyst estimates of 34 cents per share. For the full year 2025, CoreCivic expects net income to be between $53.5 million and $67.5 million, with FFO projected to be in the range of $1.37 to $1.50 per share. Jason Weaver's analysis and more details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Northern Oil and Gas plans 2025 growth despite Q4 setbacks; expects production of 130.0-135.0 MBoe per day. CEO optimistic about future amid $231-232 million Q4 expenditures.
news.summary: Northern Oil and Gas, Inc. (NYSE:NOG) released their operational update for the fourth quarter of 2024 and provided guidance for 2025. The company anticipates a production rate of 131.0–132.0 thousand barrels of oil equivalent (MBoe) per day in Q4, which is at the higher end of its annual target. They spent $27 million on Ground Game and leasehold acquisitions during the quarter. Capital expenditures for the quarter, excluding non-budgeted transactions, are expected to be in the range of $231 million to $232 million. Despite challenges in Q4 and at the start of 2025, CEO Nick O’Grady expressed satisfaction with a 25% production growth in 2024 and remains positive about the company's future prospects. Looking ahead to 2025, Northern Oil and Gas forecasts a daily production of 130.0–135.0 MBoe. Details are available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: PM to visit Vancouver on Feb 16, 2025, to meet Team Canada at 2025 Invictus Games and B.C. Task Force.
news.summary: In Ottawa on February 15, 2025, the Prime Minister is scheduled to convene with Team Canada participants at the 2025 Invictus Games in the Greater Vancouver Area, British Columbia. Media personnel are advised of a pooled photo opportunity during the meeting. Additionally, the Prime Minister will also engage with the B.C. Task Force on Trade and Economic Security during the visit.

news.headline: PM to attend Canada vs USA game at 4 Nation Face-Off in Montréal on Feb 15, 2025, with media appearance at 7:50 pm on TVA Sports.
news.summary: On February 15, 2025, in Ottawa, the Prime Minister is set to participate in the Canada versus United States game at the 4 Nation Face-Off tournament in Montréal, Quebec. The Prime Minister will be available for media appearances starting at 7:50 p.m. One notable event to look forward to is an exclusive live interview with the Prime Minister on TVA Sports. Stay tuned for more updates on this significant event.

news.headline: Ontario Lottery: Feb. 15, 2025 - Ontario 49: 7, 17, 31, 34, 38, 43. LOTTARIO jackpot $420,000. POKER LOTTO: 4-C, 4-D, 6-C, 10-C, K-S.
news.summary: On February 15, 2025, in Toronto, Ontario, the numbers drawn for the Ontario 49 lottery were 7, 17, 31, 34, 38, and 43 with a bonus number of 10. The estimated jackpot for LOTTARIO was $420,000, and the numbers drawn were 9, 16, 18, 27, 40, and 41 with a bonus number of 20. The Early Bird numbers were 17, 23, 27, and 39. In POKER LOTTO, the winning hand consisted of 4 of Clubs, 4 of Diamonds, 6 of Clubs, 10 of Clubs, and King of Spades. Legend: C = CLUB, H = HEART, S = SPADE, D = DIAMOND.

news.headline: Feb. 15, 2025 LOTTO 6/49: $5 million estimated jackpot, winning numbers 08, 10, 31, 37, 43, 49. Guaranteed prize numbers given.
news.summary: On Saturday, February 15, 2025, the estimated jackpot for LOTTO 6/49 was $5 million. The winning numbers for the Lotto 649 MAIN Draw were 08, 10, 31, 37, 43, and 49 with a Bonus Number of 34. The winning numbers for the LOTTO 6/49 Guaranteed Prize included 77242708-01, 17955295-01, 17980393-03, 18005975-01, 18021105-01, 18123290-01, 18131622-01, 44411404-09, and 46227284-01.

news.headline: Hankook presents advanced EV tire tech at 2025 Jeddah E-Prix double-header, GEN3 Evo iON Race Tire excels in demanding night race conditions.
news.summary: The recent Formula E event in Jeddah marked the debut of the innovative GEN3 Evo iON Race Tire, showcasing exceptional performance during the night races. This event, serving as the season's first night race and a double-header, concluded successfully with the tire demonstrating superior capabilities in demanding high-speed circuit conditions. The tire's excellence was evident as it navigated the challenging track with precision. Additionally, this success highlights Hankook's commitment to bolstering the global electric vehicle sector, showcasing their dedication to innovation and performance in the EV market.

news.headline: Congolese troops defeat Rwanda-backed rebels in pivotal city.
$190M in crypto linked to BitConnect Ponzi scheme seized by Indian authorities after its collapse in 2018.
news.summary: Authorities in India have confiscated cryptocurrency valued at almost $190 million linked to the infamous BitConnect Ponzi scheme, which crumbled in 2018.

news.headline: CEO Musk announced xAI’s Grok 3 Chatbot launch on Monday. Take note!
Rio Tinto's operations resume post Cyclone Zelia, no harm to people, mines active. Dampier Port closed 10 days this year. Ship loading at Cape Lambert on Feb 15.
news.summary: Rio Tinto's operations in Perth, Australia have returned to normal following Tropical Cyclone Zelia. The cyclone hit Western Australia's Pilbara region on February 14, 2025, north of Port Hedland. Fortunately, all employees remained safe during the weather event. Ship loading at Cape Lambert and Dampier Ports resumed on February 15, and all mines are now operational. Dampier Port had been closed for a total of 10 days this year, with 13 closure days so far.

news.headline: ReShape Lifesciences® priced an upsized $6.0 million public offering, selling 2,575,107 units at $2.33 each.
news.summary: In Irvine, California on February 15, 2025, ReShape Lifesciences® revealed the pricing details of its public offering. The Company (Nasdaq: RSLS) disclosed the offering of 2,575,107 units at $2.33 per unit. Each unit includes either a common share or a pre-funded warrant for one common share and one warrant for an additional common share, marking a significant step in the premier physician-led weight loss and metabolic health solutions company's financial endeavors.

news.headline: Elon Musk announces Grok 3 Chatbot launch on Monday. #xAI #Grok3 #ElonMusk
Bitcoin expert transfers Bitcoin to ETFs to avoid key issues, finds peace of mind with spot Bitcoin ETFs.
news.summary: An expert on Bitcoin recently shared that he transferred his personally-held Bitcoin assets into spot Bitcoin ETFs for a sense of security, to avoid the complexities associated with managing private keys. This decision was made to bring about peace of mind to the analyst, showcasing a shift towards utilizing ETFs. The move highlights the increasing popularity of Bitcoin ETFs, indicating a growing trend within the cryptocurrency space towards this investment vehicle. This shift could potentially pave the way for a broader adoption of ETFs in the realm of cryptocurrencies.
